title: Update header navigation: remove Social Media & Login links, add Sign Up dropdown with Login option

requirements.text: Update the Navigation component (frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx) to remove 'Social Media' from the primary navigation links entirely — it must not appear in either the desktop nav or the mobile hamburger menu.

quotes: Remove "Social Media" from the top header navigation completely.

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Social Media' link is absent from the desktop navigation bar.
The 'Social Media' link is absent from the mobile hamburger menu.
All other existing nav links (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) remain intact.

requirements.text: Remove the separate standalone 'Login' link from the Navigation component (frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx). The login option must only be accessible via the 'Sign Up' dropdown described in REQ-57, not as a top-level nav item. This applies to both the desktop nav and the mobile hamburger menu.

quotes: Remove the separate "Login" link from the main header.

acceptanceCriteria: No standalone 'Login' link exists in the desktop navigation bar.
No standalone 'Login' link exists in the mobile hamburger menu.
Removing it does not break any other navigation element.

requirements.text: In the Navigation component (frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx), replace the current 'Sign Up' and 'Login' individual links with a single golden yellow (#f5c518) primary CTA button labeled 'Sign Up' that opens a dropdown menu with exactly two options: (1) 'Sign Up' (primary option, styled prominently) which navigates to /customer-signup, and (2) 'Login' (secondary option below Sign Up) which navigates to /customer-login. The dropdown should appear on click or hover with a clean, professional dark navy (#0a1628) background and white text for the dropdown items, with golden yellow hover highlight. On desktop, position this button on the right side of the nav alongside the phone number. In the mobile hamburger menu, show the same two options (Sign Up and Login) as separate tappable rows without a dropdown — they can be rendered as direct links in the expanded mobile menu. If a customer session is active (sessionStorage key 'ymw_customer_session' is set), hide the Sign Up dropdown button entirely and instead show a 'My Account' link pointing to /customer-dashboard, consistent with the existing session-aware logic.

quotes: Keep only one primary button in the header named "Sign Up".
Create a dropdown under the "Sign Up" button with two options: Sign Up (primary option), Login (secondary option below Sign Up)
Make the Sign Up button highlighted as primary call-to-action.

acceptanceCriteria: A single 'Sign Up' button with golden yellow (#f5c518) background appears on the right side of the desktop nav.
Clicking or hovering the 'Sign Up' button opens a dropdown with two options: 'Sign Up' and 'Login'.
Clicking 'Sign Up' in the dropdown navigates to /customer-signup.
Clicking 'Login' in the dropdown navigates to /customer-login.
The dropdown has a dark navy background with white text and golden yellow hover highlight.
On mobile, the hamburger menu contains 'Sign Up' and 'Login' as direct tappable links.
If a customer session is active, the Sign Up dropdown is replaced by a 'My Account' link to /customer-dashboard.
The phone number remains visible in the header to the right of the nav links.
The 'Get Free Consultation' button (if present in the existing nav) is unaffected.

requirements.text: Ensure the final desktop Navigation layout in frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx follows this structure: Logo (YMW branding) on the far left; navigation links (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) centered in the middle; phone number (+91 8102906339) and the 'Sign Up' primary CTA button (with dropdown) on the far right. No other top-level links should appear between the centered nav links and the right-side elements. The layout must remain fully responsive — collapsing into the hamburger menu on mobile screens.

quotes: Maintain a clean and professional layout with: Logo on left, Menu in center (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact), Phone number and Sign Up button on right
Keep the phone number visible in the header.

acceptanceCriteria: Logo is on the far left of the navigation bar.
Navigation links (Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact) are centered.
Phone number and Sign Up button are on the far right.
Layout is fully responsive and collapses into a hamburger menu on mobile.
No extra top-level links appear between the centered links and the right-side elements.

constraints: Do not alter any existing navigation links: Home, Services, About, Blog, Contact must remain in the center nav.
Do not remove the phone number (+91 8102906339) from the header.
The /customer-signup and /customer-login routes must not be changed — only the nav links pointing to them are being updated.
Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
The Social Media page (/social-media) must continue to exist as a route — only the nav link is removed.
nonGoals: Changing the Social Media page itself.
Modifying the /signup (Internet Identity) page or route.
Changing any page content or layout outside the Navigation component.
